title: Buildah ignores STORAGE_DRIVER=overlay2 as environment variable
body:
**Description**

I run buildah inside docker. When I use `--storage-driver overlay2` flag buildah works as expected. If I set `STORAGE_DRIVER=overlay2` as env variable buildah fallbacks to `overlay` and tries to use `fuse-overlayfs`

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

Run

```
docker run --security-opt apparmor:unconfined --cap-add SYS_ADMIN -e STORAGE_DRIVER=overlay2 quay.io/buildah/stable /bin/bash -c "env; buildah info"
```

**Describe the results you received:**

```bash
HOSTNAME=6566d58d50e5
DISTTAG=f37container
PWD=/
container=oci
HOME=/root
FGC=f37
STORAGE_DRIVER=overlay2
BUILDAH_ISOLATION=chroot
SHLVL=1
PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin
_=/usr/bin/env
time="2023-04-19T08:15:57Z" level=warning msg="Switching default driver from overlay2 to the equivalent overlay driver"
{
    "host": {
        "CgroupVersion": "v2",
        "Distribution": {
            "distribution": "fedora",
            "version": "37"
        },
        "MemFree": 5563019264,
        "MemTotal": 8337420288,
        "OCIRuntime": "crun",
        "SwapFree": 0,
        "SwapTotal": 0,
        "arch": "amd64",
        "cpus": 8,
        "hostname": "6566d58d50e5",
        "kernel": "5.18.0-0.bpo.1-amd64",
        "os": "linux",
        "rootless": true,
        "uptime": "853h 15m 41.47s (Approximately 35.54 days)",
        "variant": ""
    },
    "store": {
        "ContainerStore": {
            "number": 0
        },
        "GraphDriverName": "overlay",
        "GraphOptions": [
            "overlay2.imagestore=/var/lib/shared",
            "overlay.mount_program=/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs",
            "overlay.mountopt=nodev,fsync=0"
        ],
        "GraphRoot": "/var/lib/containers/storage",
        "GraphStatus": {
            "Backing Filesystem": "extfs",
            "Native Overlay Diff": "false",
            "Supports d_type": "true",
            "Using metacopy": "false"
        },
        "ImageStore": {
            "number": 0
        },
        "RunRoot": "/run/containers/storage"
    }
}
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

 The same as I would run `buildah --storage-driver overlay2 info`:

```bash
{
    "host": {
        "CgroupVersion": "v2",
        "Distribution": {
            "distribution": "fedora",

        "kernel": "5.18.0-0.bpo.1-amd64",
        "os": "linux",

        "uptime": "853h 19m 36.56s (Approximately 35.54 days)",
        "variant": ""
    },
    "store": {
        "ContainerStore": {
            "number": 0
        },
        "GraphDriverName": "overlay",
        "GraphOptions": [],
        "GraphRoot": "/var/lib/containers/storage",
        "GraphStatus": {
            "Backing Filesystem": "extfs",
            "Native Overlay Diff": "true",
            "Supports d_type": "true",
            "Using metacopy": "false"
        },
        "ImageStore": {
            "number": 0
        },
        "RunRoot": "/run/containers/storage"
    }
}
```

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**


**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

```
-
```

comments.body: Hi @alvicsam , `overlay` and `overlay2` are same in the context of `c/storage`, try changing it to something else like `vfs` and see it if acknowledges or not.

Hi @flouthoc! Yes, but they work in a different way. `overlay ` does use `fuse-overlayfs` and `overlay2` doesn't. I see that `"GraphDriverName": "overlay"` is the same in both cases but `"GraphOptions"` are different. So I'm expecting that env variable `STORAGE_DRIVER=overlay2` works in the same way as flag `--storage-driver overlay2` and doesn't use `fuse-overlayfs`.
Most likely this is a string comparison issue somewhere in the stack, but does it really matter?

I think yes because with env variable `STORAGE_DRIVER=overlay2` buildah doesn't work as expected. 

As en example I can give my use case: I use buildah in CI and I want switch to overlay2. I need to go to each repo and add `--storage-driver overlay2` in each job that uses buildah. Instead I could set a global environment variable for all projects. 

But the main problem that I see here is that `buildah` behaves inconsistently.

Sure, but changing to STORAGE_DRIVER=overlay, everything works.  I am just saying that I don;t see this as a priority for us to fix, since you are using a storage driver that really should not be used.  If you want to dig into the code to find where the bug it, we would welcome a PR.

> you are using a storage driver that really should not be use

May I ask why? Modern kernels support rootless overlay2. Why should I use fuse-overlayfs which is usually used as a fallback?

overlay2 is just a string of characters. The kernel does not support overlay2. Docker had two implementations of the overlay storage driver, the newer one it called overlay2, In Podman we use the newer one only and just call it "overlay". We support an alias in storage.conf that supports overlay2 by substituting "overlay" for people who get confused.  In this case we must have missed one of the code paths when users are setting the environment variable, is my guess.  This has nothing to do with the kernel support for overlay or rootless overlay, which is what I think you are thinking is overlay2.

My major point is the people who actively work on Buildah, Podman and friends have hundreds of bugs to work on along with new features.  The problem you found is not likely to bubble up in importance to be fixed by one of these engineers.  So I advise you, if you want to fix this to open a PR or if you can't then just use the string "overlay" with the environment variable.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since this is easily worked around, I am closing this issue.
